title: Ensure Identity Federation is set up with a Corporate IDP

input_row.title: Ensure Identity Federation is set up with a Corporate IDP
input_row.description: Allow users to log in to IBM Cloud by using their corporate Identity Provider (IdP) to authenticate.
input_row.rationale: Identity Federation allows enterprises to use their enterprise Identity Provider (IdP) to integrate with IBM Cloud, and alleviates the need for enterprise users to remember a separate username and password from IBMid system. The enterprise IdP also allows your enterprise to manage user access, set log in rules, apply corporate governance policies to the log in process, and set things like password expiration rules and character requirements.
input_row.audit: Console: During log in, a federated IBM Cloud account redirects you to an external IdP that is configured during the identity federation process. API: N/A
input_row.remediation: Contact https://www.ibm.com/cloud/support to begin the identity federation process.
input_row.impact: After the account is federated, users use their corporate login credentials to access IBM Cloud.
input_row.default_value: By default, IBM Cloud accounts are not federated with an external IdP.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Navigate to the IBM Cloud console (https://cloud.ibm.com).
2. Attempt to log in with a federated IBM Cloud account. You should be redirected to the organization's configured corporate Identity Provider (IdP) during the login process.
3. Verify that you see the corporate IdP login page, indicating that identity federation is correctly set up.
4. Confirm that this login allows you to access IBM Cloud resources using your corporate login credentials.
5. Take note of the login rules, governance policies, and any password requirements related to the corporate IdP if available.
6. Review the identity federation setup by consulting the documentation at [IBM Identity Federation](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-federated_id).
7. Check with your IT department or refer to your organization's governance policies to ensure they are being applied correctly during the federation process. 

**Expected secure configuration:** Users should be able to log in using their corporate credentials, and the login process should route through the organization's configured IdP without issues.

**How to confirm compliance:** If you successfully redirect to the IdP and can authenticate using corporate credentials, it indicates compliance with this control. If you are redirected to a standard IBMid login page, federation is not correctly set up, indicating non-compliance.
